frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: Arre bhai, har aadmi ko suspect ki tarah treat karna bhi theek nahi hai. Kuch log galat hote hain, lekin sab mardon ko same category mein daal dena unfair hai — main khud itne saalon se family aur office mein hoon, kabhi aisi soch bhi nahi rakhi. Aur phir bolte hain women don't feel safe, but men also face problems outside, uska koi baat hi nahi karta.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it dismisses women's fear by centering male hurt and insisting that women's generalized caution is unfair. It encodes the 'not all men' response, self-exception as a 'good man,' and a deflection to men's safety.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Mohammed. Age 44. A Muslim man in Hyderabad. Your 22 year old daughter Sana has been offered a job in Mumbai that requires her to relocate. She is excited about it and has already told her employer she will join. She did not ask you before accepting. You are sitting with your wife after dinner and telling her what you think about Sana accepting a job offer without your permission and what you are going to tell Sana tomorrow and why a girl should not be making these kinds of decisions alone.
